name: Medplum
license: Apache-2.0 / verify hosted-service terms separately if relevant
what: Open-source healthcare developer platform built around FHIR, APIs, auth, forms, messaging, and healthcare-grade workflows.
solves: Healthcare data models, FHIR APIs, scheduling/forms/workflows, and regulated-health application development.
users: Healthcare software teams and organizations building real clinical or patient-data systems.
fit: Powerful but likely too heavy and too clinical for Hylono unless the business explicitly enters regulated health data territory. This should not be adopted casually for health-adjacent wellness operations.
runtime: Node/TypeScript healthcare platform with database, auth, FHIR APIs, object storage, and operational services.
components: FHIR server, auth, web app/components, storage, messaging/forms/workflows, and healthcare-specific APIs.
deploy: Self-host docs exist, but production expectations are much closer to healthcare platform operations than ordinary app deployment.
deps: Database, storage, auth, backups, observability, security controls, and domain expertise in healthcare data.
datastores: Relational database plus object storage and healthcare resources.
auth: Integrated auth/identity plus healthcare-facing access controls and APIs.
apis: FHIR APIs, auth, forms, bots/hooks, and developer SDKs.
security: This is PHI-capable infrastructure. Security, auditing, compliance, and incident readiness become central if used.
setup_path: Do not implement unless Hylono makes an explicit strategic decision to handle clinical/patient-grade data with the necessary governance.
env: DB settings, storage config, auth/domain settings, secrets, and healthcare integration configs.
persistence: Back up DB and object storage with healthcare-grade recovery expectations if adopted.
operations: Healthcare data governance, upgrades, auditability, and domain-model correctness are the main burden.
failure_points: Using Medplum merely because the business is health-adjacent, without true clinical-data requirements or governance readiness.
integration: Only relevant if Hylono decides to build regulated or patient-data workflows. Otherwise it is overkill.
verify: Decide whether Hylono will ever store PHI/regulated healthcare data. If not, Medplum is likely unnecessary now.
risk: Very high compliance and operational burden.
gdpr: If used for health data, risk level rises sharply; GDPR plus health-data special-category rules apply.
recommendation: DEFER UNLESS EXPLICIT HEALTHCARE DATA PROGRAM EXISTS
overlap: Could overlap with Metriport/FHIR ambitions, but all of those are unnecessary unless true healthcare data enters scope.
codex_read_first: Read the health-data strategy decision and compliance notes before considering Medplum.
codex_best_path: Do not implement without explicit stakeholder decision that PHI/clinical-grade workflows are in scope.
codex_never_assume: Never treat health-adjacent wellness operations as equivalent to a healthcare software mandate.

assets.name: Self-host best practices
assets.notes: Useful for seeing real ops expectations.
